output.change: mt-8 moved from outer wrapper to .header-main in both TSX and PHP

fix1_outerWrapperNoMt8.description: Outer wrapper no longer has mt-8 in both TSX and PHP
fix1_outerWrapperNoMt8.detail: TSX line 472: className string is now 'relative flex flex-col gap-2' (mt-8 absent). PHP line 42: class key is now 'relative flex flex-col gap-2' (mt-8 absent). Both files confirmed clean.

fix2_headerMainHasMt8.description: .header-main element now has mt-8 in both TSX and PHP
fix2_headerMainHasMt8.detail: TSX line 498: base class string is 'header-main bg-charcoal rounded-[25px] px-8 py-4 w-full mt-8 transition-[transform,opacity]'. PHP line 59: base class string is 'header-main bg-charcoal rounded-[25px] px-8 py-4 w-full mt-8 transition-[transform,opacity]'. Both match exactly.

fix3_frontendTopMargin.description: Frontend renders correctly — nav bar pill still has top margin
fix3_frontendTopMargin.detail: At 1440px the nav bar pill visually has clear top spacing from the demo container edge in all normal-mode variations. The mt-8 on .header-main produces the same visual result as before — spacing between the container top edge and the rounded nav bar. No double-spacing artifacts visible.

fix4_overlayMode.description: Overlay mode still works on frontend
fix4_overlayMode.detail: The 'DISPLAY ABOVE CONTENT (OVERLAY MODE)' section visible in all breakpoint screenshots. The header bar overlays the Lorem ipsum paragraphs below — content starts at the top of the wrapper container without being pushed down by the header height. The .header-main absolute positioning combined with the -mb-[var(--header-main-height,0px)] on the outer wrapper continues to function correctly. mt-8 on .header-main correctly offsets the overlay header from the very top of the container.

fix5_normalMode.detail: 9 out of 10 variations are non-overlay. All render with proper spacing and structure across all four breakpoints and three browsers. No layout regressions observed.

fix6_fixedOnScroll.description: Fixed-on-scroll still works in both modes
fix6_fixedOnScroll.detail: Moving mt-8 from the outer wrapper to .header-main does not affect scroll behaviour. The JS scroll listener targets .header-main by class name and applies/removes 'scrolling', 'scrolling-up', 'scrolling-down' classes. The data attributes (data-fixed-scroll-desktop, data-fixed-scroll-mobile, etc.) remain on .header-main unchanged. The fixed-position CSS modifiers ([&.scrolling]:fixed) are still part of .header-main's conditional class list. No code changes to scroll logic were made.
fix6_fixedOnScroll.note: Scroll behaviour is code-path verified; runtime scroll testing requires manual browser interaction beyond automated screenshot capability.

passedChecks: TSX outer wrapper class: 'relative flex flex-col gap-2' — mt-8 absent (line 472)
PHP outer wrapper class: 'relative flex flex-col gap-2' — mt-8 absent (line 42)
TSX and PHP .header-main base strings are identical
Frontend nav bar has correct top margin at all breakpoints
Overlay mode visually confirmed — content not pushed down
No layout regressions in any browser at any breakpoint

visualObservations.375px: Mobile layout correct across all variations. Header bar pill has visible top margin above it inside each demo container. SEP mobile button appears above nav bar when configured. Overlay variation near bottom shows header floating over Lorem ipsum text with content starting from top of container. Footer renders correctly.
visualObservations.768px: Tablet (below 960px breakpoint) — mobile layout with hamburger toggle. All 10 variations present. Header bar margin consistent. Overlay variation confirmed.
visualObservations.1024px: Desktop layout (above 960px). Full utility row with search and SEP link visible. Nav items horizontal. All variations correct. Overlay section clearly shows header over Lorem ipsum paragraphs — paragraphs begin immediately under the header without gap.
visualObservations.1440px: Full desktop. All 10 variations visible with consistent mt-8 spacing on the nav bar pill. The 'DISPLAY ABOVE CONTENT (OVERLAY MODE)' variation confirmed: header bar sits absolutely over the content with Lorem ipsum text starting from the container top edge, not below the header.
